data.title: Cathie Wood Doubles Down on High-Potential Biotech Plays
data.description:
Cathie Wood's ARK Invest ETFs (exchange-traded funds) made notable portfolio moves on Monday, January 12, as revealed in daily fund disclosures. The trades underscore ARK's ongoing focus on disruptive innovation in biotech, autonomous driving, blockchain, and fintech, while trimming slower-growth holdings.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Wood's Major Biotech Buys

ARK's largest purchase was 133,191 shares of GeneDx Holdings (WGS) for $15.88 million through the ARK Innovation ETF (ARKK) and the ARK Genomic Revolution ETF (ARKG). GeneDx is a global leader in rare disease diagnosis with the largest such genomic dataset. WGS shares plunged 11.9% yesterday, after the company's guidance disappointed investors, despite robust preliminary FY25 results.

Similarly, Wood added 99,292 shares of clinical-stage biotech firm Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) for about $1.13 million, despite a 10.2% surge. She also added 49,963 shares of 10X Genomics (TXG), capitalizing on the 3.2% dip in its stock price. Finally, Wood bought 17,748 shares of Personalis (PSNL), consistent with her recent buying streak.

Wood Sells Mature Biotech Holdings

Wood offloaded 91,312 shares of Illumina (ILMN) for $13.29 million, signaling a shift toward earlier-stage genomics plays over established names. ILMN shares have surged nearly 48% over the past six months.

Additionally, Wood trimmed exposure to Natera (NTRA) by $2.61 million, Teradyne (TER) by $5.15 million, Beam Therapeutics (BEAM) by $1.11 million, and a minor sale in Ionis Pharmaceuticals (IONS).

Wood's Strategic Portfolio Shuffle

Let's see how some of these stocks perform using the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool:

Currently, analysts have a "Strong Buy" consensus rating on most of these stocks, with GeneDx stock offering the highest upside potential over the next twelve months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: Tesla Stock (NASDAQ:TSLA) Notches Up Despite Plummeting Cybertruck Sales Figures
data.description:
Electric vehicle giant Tesla (TSLA) has an ambitious schedule afoot for 2026. With some referring to it as Tesla's "Prove-It Year," it has plans to roll out everything from robotics to Roadsters. And as more information emerges about 2025, we discover just how vital those plans really are. Tesla's Cybertruck is not selling well. This left investors cautiously optimistic, as Tesla shares gained fractionally in Tuesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The numbers that emerged are troubling. Cox Automotive data says that Tesla sold just 20,237 Cybertrucks in the United States in all of 2025. That is about half of what Tesla sold back in 2024, and the numbers only got worse the farther they went. The fourth quarter of 2025 saw sales drop 68%. And given that Elon Musk was out to sell over 10 times that number at 250,000 a year, it is clear the Cybertruck is not the sales powerhouse some had hoped for.

While there were plenty of reservations for the Cybertruck, they did not pan out into sales. It did not help that the original price for a Cybertruck was supposed to be $40,000, but ballooned to nearly double that before launch. Throw in 10 different recalls in two years—including one nightmarish possibility of an accelerator pedal that could get stuck at full—and the strange-looking, occasionally dangerous almost-pickup just did not work.

Who Can Dethrone Tesla?

Thus, some are looking to who could ultimately take Tesla's place in the market. Some companies are clear possibilities. Other electric vehicle makers might qualify, like XPeng (XPEV) or even Ford (F), who recently rolled out plans for electric vehicles with the least expensive motors around.

But one report suggests the real Tesla dethroner is none other than Nvidia (NVDA). The launch of Alpamayo represents a significant threat to Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) concept. It has the potential, reports note, to become the Android to Tesla's iOS. If businesses are willing to license Alpamayo over FSD, then Nvidia could indeed become a Tesla-killer, on at least one front.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 13.27% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $401.93 per share implies 10.16% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: JPMorgan Chase (JPM) Says the U.S. Federal Reserve Is Likely to Raise Interest Rates
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (JPM) expects that the U.S. Federal Reserve's next move will be to increase interest rates rather than lower them.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

and unlikely to occur before third-quarter 2027, JPMorgan predicted, bucking some crypto analysts' outlook for lower borrowing costs, sooner.

The world's largest bank by market capitalization said on Friday it expects the Fed to hold rates steady with a 3.5%-3.75% target this year and raise by 25 basis points in the third quarter of 2027, according to Reuters.

This is in stark contrast to pricing in the CME's fed fund futures, which show traders positioned for two 25 basis-point rate cuts this year. Many crypto analysts also say they expect borrowing costs to decline, incentivizing greater risk-taking across the economy and financial markets. Bitcoin
BTC
$93,238.51
, often considered a pure play on fiat liquidity, is more sensitive to interest-rate expectations than traditional assets.

"Despite a difficult 2025, bitcoin may stage a comeback in 2026," Lukman Otunuga, senior market analyst at FXTM, said in an email to CoinDesk. "Lower interest rates and a thinning active supply could support prices."

Most crypto bulls expect the next Fed Chairman to be more dovish than the incumbent, Jerome Powell, whose term is set to end in early May.

JPMorgan's prediction of higher rates aligns with bullish chart patterns in the 10-year Treasury yield CoinDesk discussed in November. The pattern suggests the benchmark bond yield could rise toward 6% in the coming year or so. It's currently about 4.18%.

The bank, however, maintained that rate cuts could be back on the table if the labor market weakens or inflation falls.

"If the labor market weakens again in the coming months, or if inflation falls materially, the Fed could still ease later this year," analysts at JPMorgan said.

"However, we expect the labor market to tighten by the second quarter and the disinflation process to be quite gradual."

Several other investment banks have reassessed their rate-cut forecasts following Friday's U.S. employment data, which showed the jobless rate dipped to 4.4% in December.

Goldman Sachs and Barclays now foresee rate reductions in September and December, following a lowering in June, compared with their previous projections of cuts in March and June.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Defense Secretary Praises SpaceX as Trump Pushes for Faster Military Innovation
data.description:
SpaceX is becoming a clear beneficiary of the Trump administration's push to speed up military innovation, as Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth publicly praised the aerospace company during a visit to its Starbase launch site in Texas. Indeed, the visit showed how much Elon Musk's relationship with the White House has improved and how important SpaceX has become to U.S. defense plans. It also highlighted how the government is turning more toward private companies to develop advanced technology faster.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In addition, Hegseth criticized traditional defense contractors for being too cautious and slow to innovate. He said that the Pentagon plans to use Musk's Grok AI platform across military systems as part of its goal to become an "AI-first warfighting force." This includes using AI on both classified and unclassified networks. His comments also matched President Donald Trump's recent call for a $500 billion increase in defense spending, along with Trump's warning that slow-moving contractors could lose future contracts.

Interestingly, SpaceX already plays a major role in U.S. government programs, as it holds about $4 billion in NASA contracts and launches sensitive national security satellites using its Falcon 9 and Falcon Heavy rockets. Although Musk continues to talk about long-term goals like space exploration beyond Earth, the visit made it clear that SpaceX's work is now more in line with U.S. military priorities.

What Is the Prediction for TSLA Stock?

When it comes to Elon Musk's companies, most of them are privately held. However, retail investors can invest in his most popular company, Tesla (TSLA). Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 11 Holds, and seven Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average TSLA price target of $401.93 per share implies 10.8% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: Trump Pressures Powell to Slash Rates Following 'Great (LOW!) Inflation numbers'
data.description:
President Trump reiterated his call for Fed Chair Jerome Powell to cut rates on Tuesday after December's inflation data remained steady. The annual growth of both the Consumer Price Index (CPI) and core CPI remained unchanged from November, coming in at 2.7% and 2.6%, respectively.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"JUST OUT: Great (LOW!) Inflation numbers for the USA," Trump said in a Truth Social post on Tuesday. "That means that Jerome 'Too Late' Powell should cut interest rates, MEANINGFULLY!!!"

Rate Cut Odds Drop amid DOJ Investigation of Powell

However, inflation remains stubbornly above the Fed's target of 2%. In addition, the odds of the Fed holding rates steady at the January 28, March 18, and April 29 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings all ticked higher following the inflation data, according to CME's FedWatch tool.

Powell's term as Fed Chair is set to end in May. Over the weekend, he announced that he was subject to a Department of Justice (DOJ) investigation over the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. In a video statement, he argued the true nature of the investigation centers on the Fed making policy decisions independently instead of adhering to pressure from Trump to lower rates.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Nike (NKE) Signs Endorsement Deal With Pickleball Athlete
data.description:
In what's being called a first, sneaker giant Nike (NKE) has singed an endorsement deal with a pickleball player.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Specifically, Nike has signed a deal with No. 1-ranked pickleball player Anna Leigh Waters, making her the first pickleball athlete to join Nike's roster of stars. The sports apparel company has long been associated with professional athletes such as golfer Tiger Woods and basketball player Michael Jordan.

The signing of a pickleball player is a first for Nike and indicates how the sport, which is similar to tennis, has grown in popularity and become more mainstream in recent years. Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed. However, Nike said that Waters will represent Nike for both apparel and footwear across all of her events and professional pickleball tournaments.

Who Is Anna Leigh Waters?

Anna Leigh Waters is an 18-year-old American who is currently the number one ranked woman's pickleball player in the world. Nike said she will also serve as a global pickleball ambassador for company. Waters previously had a footwear deal with the sports company Fila that expired in 2025.

Pickleball has seen explosive growth in recent years, but up until now, Nike has been slow to jump on the trend. The company is currently in the midst of a turnaround plan under new CEO Elliott Hill after lagging sales and a stock slump.

The new partnership caps off a record-breaking year for Waters. She is currently ranked No. 1 in women's singles, doubles and mixed doubles, with a record 181 gold medals and 39 career triple crowns (winning singles, doubles and mixed doubles at one event). Waters' pickleball career began in 2019, when she became the youngest-ever pro pickleball player at the age of 12.

″Growing up, I watched my idols wear the Swoosh in their biggest moments, so joining the Nike family is a dream realized," Waters in a statement. "Nike is the gold standard in performance and I can't wait to step onto the court representing a brand that believes in the same relentless pursuit of greatness that drives me every day."

Last week, Waters announced she was switching paddle companies and joining Franklin Sports. Waters also has deals with major brands including Delta Air Lines
, DoorDash
and Ulta Beauty
. She currently makes $2 million annually from the United Pickleball Association, according to a person familiar with Waters' earnings, who asked to remain anonymous to speak about nonpublic matters.

Her new Nike partnership will debut this week as she competes at the Carvana Masters in Rancho Mirage, California, according to a representative for Waters.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Construction Group EquipmentShare Set for $775M U.S. IPO
data.description:
Construction technology company EquipmentShare.com is planning to raise $777.75 million in its upcoming U.S. IPO.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The group said today that it had applied to list on the Nasdaq Global Select Market under the ticker symbol "EQPT."

Mission to Reshape Construction

It plans to price 30.5 million shares of Class A common stock between $23.50 and $25.50 per share. Goldman Sachs (GS) & Co. LLC, Wells Fargo Securities (WFC), and UBS Investment Bank (UBS) are serving as lead book-running managers for the offering, alongside Citigroup (C) and Guggenheim Securities.

The company was founded in 2015 by brothers Jabbok and Willy Schlacks with the aim of reshaping the construction sector. It is headquartered in Columbia, Missouri, and has now grown to become one of the largest equipment rental providers in the United States, with 373 locations across 45 states as of 2025.

In December, the group celebrated the construction of its new Roswell branch and announced a broader statewide expansion that will bring 5 to 10 new locations to communities across New Mexico.

It offers a range of solutions for the construction industry including its self-built T3 technology platform, which provides fleet management, telematics devices, and an equipment rental marketplace. It is designed to increase productivity and efficiency in the construction sector.

In November, it was ranked No. 4 on The Software Report's Top 50 Software Companies of 2025. It highlights companies advancing software innovation and real-world impact across multiple industries.

Huge Market Opportunity

The global construction equipment rental market size is calculated at $113.61 billion in 2025 and is predicted to increase from $118.38 billion in 2026 to approximately $175.21 billion by 2035. Lower interest rates will help as will growth in demand for major projects such as data centers.

The EquipmentShare IPO marks an impressive start to 2026 for new U.S. listings. L3Harris (LHX) is looking to IPO its robotics missile business with bitcoin miner BitGo also looking to go public. Other big names which might list this year include Anthropic and OpenAI.

What Other IPOs are in the Pipeline?

data.author: David Craik

data.title: Trump Says 'HELP IS ON ITS WAY' for Iranian Protestors
data.description:
President Trump issued a rallying call for Iranian protesters on Tuesday, urging them to keep up the fight and to "TAKE OVER YOUR INSTITUTIONS!!!" Trump also said that he would cancel all meetings with Iranian officials until the bloodshed against protesters ends.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"I have cancelled all meetings with Iranian Officials until the senseless killing of protesters STOPS," Trump said in a Truth Social post. "HELP IS ON ITS WAY. MIGA!!!"

Trump Hits Iran With Tariffs, Considers Other Options

Trump has been briefed on several options to address the situation in Iran, including long-range missile strikes and digital operations. On Monday, he announced a 25% secondary tariff on countries that trade with Iran. Tehran has warned that it would retaliate if attacked by the U.S.

Over 1,800 protesters have been killed in Iran since the uprising began on December 28, according to the Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA). The protests initially addressed economic issues, such as high inflation, before expanding to challenge political repression.

data.title: Trump's Iran Tariff Move Strains U.S.-China Trade Truce
data.description:
On Monday, President Trump announced a 25% tariff on all countries that transact business with Iran in response to its treatment of anti-regime protestors. That could complicate the U.S.-China trade truce.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"China firmly opposes any illicit unilateral sanctions and long-arm jurisdiction, and will take all necessary measures to safeguard its legitimate rights and interests," said Chinese Embassy spokesperson Liu Pengyu in an X post following the announcement.

China's Iran Trade Exposure Raises Tariff Uncertainty

China is Iran's largest trading partner and purchased over 80% of its oil exports in 2025, according to data firm Kpler. Iran's other major trading partners include India, Russia, and Turkey.

Trump didn't immediately specify when the secondary tariffs would take effect or if the 25% rate would be stacked on top of China's existing tariff rate of 45% for most goods. Last year, he imposed a similar tariff hike on India for buying Russian oil.

data.title: Google to Develop, Manufacture High-End Smartphones in Vietnam, Phasing Out China
data.description:
Alphabet's (GOOGL) Google unit is planning to develop and manufacture its high-end smartphones in Vietnam this year, marking a phased exit from China. The company will start conducting new product introductions (NPI) for its Pixel, Pixel Pro, and Pixel Fold phones there. Meanwhile, development of the lower-end Pixel A series will continue in China for the time being, according to sources cited by Nikkei Asia.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Google's step mirrors rival Apple's (AAPL) expansion into India, as American firms slowly diversify their supply chains away from China. Google already has a large supplier network in Vietnam that assembles its products, including Pixel smartphones. Hence, developing a smartphone from scratch in Vietnam should not be as challenging.

Why Is NPI Important?

NPI is one of the most important stages in smartphone manufacturing. It involves developing, verifying, and fine-tuning production processes for new electronic devices, involving hundreds of engineers from the company and suppliers. NPI demands heavy investment in testing equipment and tooling machines to ensure designs meet exact specifications.

Suppliers see NPI participation as a badge of honor, signaling their ability to handle a client's latest products. Yet, both Apple and Google still run NPIs in China, despite having worked for years to diversify supply chains, given the process' complexity in less mature ecosystems.  

Google Exits China to Dodge Tariff Issues

Google's moves come amid growing uncertainty over the Trump administration's tariff policies, which have rocked supply chains since April 2025. Apple, for instance, has ramped up capacity in India and Vietnam. Apple's NPI needs around 200-300 engineers at the supplier's plant, signaling huge investment. If NPI fails, no new product will be launched that year, but fortunately this has never happened. The focus is on functionality, compatibility, and reliability for mass production.

Apple reportedly runs dual NPIs in India and China as backups, since the stakes are high. This doubles resources to avoid failure. If Google and Apple succeed outside China, it would be a big win in diminishing reliance on the world's manufacturing hub.

Challenges in Shifting from China

Moving key manufacturing development from China is just one step, and expanding smartphone production overseas is hard. Beijing blocks exports of equipment and relocation of Chinese workers. For example, Apple suppliers face stricter Chinese customs checks on equipment exports, slowing iPhone expansion in India. Google's Vietnam smartphone capacity plans last year also fell behind for the same reasons, two sources stated.

China makes tons of production and testing equipment, but Beijing makes it hard to export them, fearing loss of manufacturing edge.

Is GOOGL Stock a Buy?

Analysts remain highly optimistic about Alphabet's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, GOOGL stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 27 Buys and seven Hold ratings. The average Alphabet price target of $338.25 implies 1.9% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, GOOGL shares have surged 73.7%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth
